summary: Iteration 4 — backend-only regression after the defaults.py / storage.py adapter / CORS+cookie env refactor. Created a comprehensive new pytest suite (/app/backend/tests/test_refactor_full_regression.py, 19 tests) that covers every item in the review_request: health, /api/content/{homepage,interiors} defaults including 1BHK/2BHK/3BHK/4BHK keys, banks (>=8 including SBI + HDFC Bank), amenities (>=30 across categories), Bangalore localities (>=15), full auth lifecycle (login cookie HttpOnly+SameSite, /auth/me via Bearer excludes _id+password_hash, /auth/me via cookie-only, logout clears cookies and invalidates /me), listings, projects (with amenity_ids+bank_ids+units), universal search, public inquiry POST + 24h duplicate prevention (409), admin PUT /api/content/homepage deep_merge persistence + non-clobbering of sibling keys, CORS preflight ACAO header, /api/upload (Emergent storage backend) authenticated non-500, admin route gating (admin 200 / customer 403), and project-by-slug microsite. All 19 new tests PASS. Re-ran the full backend test suite end-to-end: 94/94 tests pass (existing 50 in test_homesqre_backend.py + 15 in test_homesqre_new_features.py + 10 in test_refactor_regression.py + 19 new). Refactor preserves all previous behaviour. Observed only one minor data-drift artefact (seed inserts 12 listings but 11 are currently `status=live`, 2 extra `pending` listings exist from earlier test runs — NOT a refactor regression; documented in test as `>=11` tolerance).

minor.issue: Seed claims 12 listings but only 11 are status=live (13 total in DB: 11 live + 2 pending). Likely caused by earlier iteration test data; not a refactor regression. If a fully clean seed is expected, consider an idempotent reseed that re-flips seeded listings back to status=live on startup, or filter seeded listings by a `seeded=true` marker.

Duplicate-prevention keys on `mobile` (not email/phone). Field is named `mobile` in DB/response but several FE forms historically used `phone`. If the FE submits {phone: ...}, mobile will be empty and dedup will never fire (since first row also has empty mobile and matches everything). Confirm FE sends `mobile`.
5 projects returned vs 4 seeded — one is a stub with slug='x' from a prior test run. Harmless but worth cleaning.

action_items: OPTIONAL: ensure FE inquiry forms POST `mobile` (not `phone`), otherwise the 24h duplicate-prevention guard is bypassed.
OPTIONAL: clean up the leftover stub project with slug='x' and the 2 pending-status listings created in prior test iterations.

OPTIONAL: as iteration_3 already flagged — keep the FE Interiors.jsx hero?.backgrounds guard and CostMatrixCell range coercion fixes (those are frontend issues, out of scope for this backend-only iteration but still worth retesting in the next FE pass).
critical_code_review_comments: defaults.py extraction is clean: SEED_BANKS / SEED_AMENITIES / BANGALORE_LOCALITIES / DEFAULT_HOMEPAGE_CONTENT / DEFAULT_INTERIORS_CONTENT all import and surface correctly through the seed_data() flow and the public content endpoints — no duplicate DEFAULT_*_CONTENT blocks remain in server.py.

storage.py adapter loads, get_storage() returns a backend with .put/.get, and /api/upload as authenticated admin does NOT 500 with STORAGE_BACKEND=emergent — good. Recommend a follow-up unit test that exercises the `local` backend path too (set STORAGE_BACKEND=local in a test fixture) to lock the migration target before the Hostinger move.

Cookie attributes are env-driven: with COOKIE_SAMESITE=lax and COOKIE_SECURE=false the Set-Cookie header correctly emits `samesite=lax` and omits `secure`, while HttpOnly is unconditionally present. Login + cookie-only /me round-trip works.

CORS: with CORS_ORIGINS=* the preflight OPTIONS for /api/listings returns ACAO=* on an arbitrary origin and credentials are disabled (correct — wildcard origin with credentials would be rejected by browsers). When CORS_ORIGINS is set to an explicit list and credentials=true, please add a separate test fixture in a future iteration.

Inquiry duplicate guard keys on `mobile` only. If `mobile` is empty for both rows the guard still fires (matches empty string), which is actually OK — but means FE must always send a non-empty mobile or every empty-mobile inquiry will be blocked after the first. Consider keying on `(listing_id|project_id) + (mobile OR email)` and require at least one to be non-empty.

Listings seed is non-idempotent (only inserts when count==0) — once one row drifts to a non-live status, the seed never repairs it. Same for projects. For a deploy-and-migrate workflow this is probably fine, but flag it for the migration runbook.
test_credentials: Used seeded creds from /app/memory/test_credentials.md (admin@homesqre.com / Homesqre@2026, customer@homesqre.com / Customer@2026).
seed_data_creation: Each run creates one inquiry row with TEST_inq_* email and a random mobile, used to verify 24h duplicate-prevention (409). One CMS homepage hero.headline marker write is performed and then restored to the prior value in the same test. Both are non-destructive.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression for the modularization+CORS/cookie refactor is GREEN — 94/94 pytest passes against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. To re-run: `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://crm-phase-tracker.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v` (~22s). The new authoritative file is test_refactor_full_regression.py. If main agent later wires STORAGE_BACKEND=local, add a fixture that flips the env var and re-runs test_upload_authenticated_does_not_500 to verify the local-filesystem branch of storage.py. The two frontend regressions flagged in iteration_3 (Interiors.jsx hero guard + CostMatrixCell range coercion) are still open and need a frontend pass before declaring the refactor fully shipped.
rca of the issue: No backend bugs uncovered in this iteration. The two transient test-script failures during authoring were RCA-d as: (a) FE field naming — server stores `mobile` not `phone`, so my initial test payload {phone: ...} caused the dup-prevention guard to miss because the matched `mobile` field was empty on both rows but the unique email I sent meant the find_one matched neither (the dup query is mobile-only + listing_id + 24h, not email). Fixed by switching the payload to `mobile`. (b) Project slug — first project in the listing had slug='x' (stale test stub) so by-slug routing wasn't expressive enough; fixed by skipping single-char slugs and using `city_slug`/`locality_slug` when present.
